pattern: Once conflicts only depend on relative age and score, sorting players by age (ties by score) turns 'no younger player may outscore an older one' into a longest weakly-increasing-score-subsequence-sum problem.
hints: Conflicts only compare a pair of players, so think about picking an order for the players that makes 'no conflict' equivalent to a single simple rule about score as you scan left to right.
Sort the players by age ascending, breaking ties by score ascending; a valid team is then any subsequence of this order whose scores never strictly decrease.

Let dp[i] be the best total score of a valid team that ends by including player i; dp[i] = scores[i] + the best dp[j] among earlier players j whose score does not exceed player i's score, and the answer is the largest dp value overall.

edge_inputs.description: everyone can be picked, ages and scores both increasing
best team excludes the lowest scorer
best team is a prefix, the last player would conflict
same age for everyone, so any combination is conflict-free regardless of score
score strictly decreases as age strictly increases: every pair conflicts, only one player can be kept
an older player has a lower score than several younger ones, but is still needed to link two score tiers

stress.description: random scores and ages up to the max size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    scores = [rng.randint(1, 1000000) for _ in range(n)]
    ages = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [scores, ages]
